blurb: ReplayKit enables screen recording and live broadcasting by performing all capture operations within a privileged system daemon (replayd) running outside the host app's process. This architecture ensures recorded video and audio content is never directly accessible to the hosting application. User consent is collected via a system-presented permissions dialog before each recording session. Third-party broadcast services integrate through a two-process extension architecture – a UI extension for configuration and an upload extension for data handling – connected to the capture daemon via XPC, with video frames serialized as IOSurface objects to prevent raw buffer access across trust boundaries.
